name: Double Nut Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Glorious Liqueurs, Mary Aurea Morris
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Lightly grease two cookie sheets with softened butter and dust with flour, shaking off the excess.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, ground almonds, sugar, eggs, rum, liqueur, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and baking powder. Beat until well blended.
Stir in the walnuts and whole almonds. The dough should be soft and slightly sticky but should hold its shape when picked up. If too runny, add more flour.
Scoop half of the dough into a long cylinder on one cookie sheet so it does not touch the edge. Repeat with the remaining dough.
Bake for 45 to 60 minutes or until golden brown and firm. They will spread during baking.
Remove from oven to wire rack. Let stand for 15 to 20 minutes.
Loosen cylinders with metal spatulas, let almost reach room temperature on cutting board.
Cut each cylinder into crosswise slices about 1-inch thick, cut side up.
Place sliced cookies back on cookie sheets, cut side up. Return to oven for 10 to 15 minutes until slightly golden and cool completely before storing.
